items.title: Condemning Unfair Treatment Of Master’s Trained Specialists — Doctor
items.summary: The Master’s pathway should not become the pathway that demanded more, only to be told that it counts for less. MOH and JPA must stop punishing doctors for complying with their own training arrangements. The post Condemning Unfair Treatment Of Master’s Trained Specialists — Doctor appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Why Price Controls Over Private Hospitals Is A Bad Idea — Dr James Jeremiah
items.summary: Price control may protect inefficient hospitals. When the government fixes prices, weaker hospitals don't need to innovate; stronger hospitals can't differentiate properly; and patients lose the natural benefit of market discipline. The post Why Price Controls Over Private Hospitals Is A Bad Idea — Dr James Jeremiah appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Could Social Media Affect Who We Are?
items.summary: New research suggests that long-term Instagram use may influence the way people experience their own identity and distinguish themselves from others. The post Could Social Media Affect Who We Are? appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Pharmacists Say Credentialing, Legal Reform Needed Before Prescribing Expansion
items.summary: Pharmacists back micro-credentialing and a bigger role in patient care, as they already make many clinical decisions. But Malaysia needs clear training, credentialing and governance frameworks before expanding prescribing powers to pharmacists like the UK. The post Pharmacists Say Credentialing, Legal Reform Needed Before Prescribing Expansion appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Joint Ministerial Committee Touts Base MHIT Pilot By End July
items.summary: In response to the PAC's report, the Joint Ministerial Committee says the base MHIT product will be piloted by the end of July, claiming that "significant progress" has been made across its Reset strategy, including a phased adoption of DRG. The post Joint Ministerial Committee Touts Base MHIT Pilot By End July appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Pig Farm Numbers Fall 22%, KPKM Says Pork Supply Still Sufficient
items.summary: Malaysia’s registered pig farms fell 22% and pork production declined, but Agriculture Minister Mat Sabu says supply remains sufficient as imports can cover any shortfall. The ministry also targets higher chili and ginger self-sufficiency under the 13MP. The post Pig Farm Numbers Fall 22%, KPKM Says Pork Supply Still Sufficient appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: APHM Says Joint Ministerial Committee Addressing PAC Report
items.summary: APHM says key pain points in the PAC's report on health insurance and private hospital charges are being addressed through the Joint Ministerial Committee, adding that it has engaged MOH to improve hospital bill transparency in private hospitals. The post APHM Says Joint Ministerial Committee Addressing PAC Report appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: MCA: Specify ROI Of Government’s 50 Overseas Trips
items.summary: MCA tells the government to show the ROI of each of its 50 overseas trips undertaken in 50 days, besides specifying costs and delegations, saying if official travel is justified as a "necessary investment", similar investments must be made in health care. The post MCA: Specify ROI Of Government’s 50 Overseas Trips appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: KPKM: Pre-Conflict Stock Keeps Fertiliser Price Hikes Below Global Levels
items.summary: KPKM says fertiliser prices in Malaysia rose by up to 29% but are still cheaper than international markets because suppliers are still selling pre-conflict stock. Malaysia also relies heavily on Argentina for soybean meal and grain corn for livestock feed. The post KPKM: Pre-Conflict Stock Keeps Fertiliser Price Hikes Below Global Levels appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: MOH: 351 Medical, Dental Specialists Quit Government For Private In Three Years
items.summary: MOH says 351 medical and dental specialists left the public sector for private practice from 2023 to 2025, averaging at 117 resignations a year. The main reasons cited included personal reasons, private-sector jobs, lecturer offers, and health issues. The post MOH: 351 Medical, Dental Specialists Quit Government For Private In Three Years appeared first on CodeBlue .
